title: koeleria pyramidata subsp. montana
intro: Koeleria pyramidata subsp. montana, also known as mountain hair grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the Koeleria genus, characterized by its compact growth habit and delicate panicles. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Koeleria pyramidata subsp. montana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support digestive health and reduce inflammation. Koeleria pyramidata subsp. montana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
